attribution: Wikipedia contributors — NASCAR Cup Series race articles (2022–2025).
transcription_notes: Finish position taken directly from each race's 'Official race results' wikitable on its Wikipedia article.

DNF inferred heuristically: car credited with < 90% of winner's lap count is marked dnf=true (raw laps + winner_laps preserved for audit). Wikipedia 2022-2025 Cup race tables do not include an explicit 'Status' column.

DNS is not directly observable from Wikipedia race tables (only starters appear). All dns counts are therefore 0; if a pool driver missed a race, they simply have no result entry for that event_id rather than a 'DNS' marker.
Pool driver name matching strips trailing rookie/ineligible markers like '(R)' and '(i)'. 'Daniel Suarez' aliased to 'Daniel Suárez'.
Coca-Cola 600 results overlap with the dedicated Charlotte snapshot — intentional, intermediate-form aggregates include them.

intermediate_filter_policy.definition: Intermediate = 1.5-mile oval Cup track configuration only.
included_tracks: Charlotte Motor Speedway (oval / Coca-Cola 600 only — fall Roval excluded)

excluded_event_classes.reason: Reconfigured in 2022 to draft/superspeedway-style racing; no longer functions as a traditional intermediate.
Road course infield+oval hybrid, not an intermediate oval.

excluded_event_classes.class: Other road courses (COTA, Sonoma, Watkins Glen, Chicago Street, Mexico, Indy Road Course)

excluded_event_classes.reason: Restrictor-plate / draft style, distinct from intermediate handling tracks.

excluded_event_classes.class: Short tracks (Martinsville, Richmond, Bristol, Phoenix, North Wilkesboro, LA Coliseum)

Non-1.5-mi intermediates (Darlington 1.366 mi, Pocono 2.5 mi, Indianapolis Brickyard oval 2.5 mi, Michigan 2.0 mi, Auto Club 2.0 mi pre-reconfig, New Hampshire 1.058 mi, Nashville 1.33 mi, Gateway 1.25 mi, Iowa 0.875 mi)

excluded_event_classes.reason: Non-points exhibition; excluded from points-style aggregates.
Snapshot scoped to Cup Series only.
